seoTitle: Infineon Solutions | EV Charger, Motor Drive, Solar Inverter | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Complete Infineon-based solutions for EV charging, industrial motor drives, solar inverters, wind power, and rail transit. BOM lists, schematics, design support.

faqs.question: What types of solutions does Infineon offer for industrial applications?
faqs.answer: Infineon provides comprehensive semiconductor solutions for industrial applications: Motor Drive Solutions - Complete power and control solutions for AC/DC motor drives, servo systems, and variable frequency drives using IGBT modules, gate drivers, and XMC MCUs; Power Supply Solutions - High-efficiency designs for switch-mode power supplies (SMPS), DC-DC converters, and uninterruptible power supplies (UPS) using CoolMOS, OptiMOS, and CoolSiC devices; Renewable Energy Solutions - Solar inverters, wind power converters, and energy storage systems using high-power IGBT modules and SiC MOSFETs; Industrial Automation Solutions - Factory automation, robotics, and process control using AURIX and XMC microcontrollers with integrated safety features. Each solution includes recommended product combinations, reference designs, and technical documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore the specific solution categories below to find detailed information about products, benefits, and implementation guidance for your application.

faqs.question: How do I choose between different motor drive solutions?
faqs.answer: Motor drive solution selection depends on power level and application: Low Power (0.5-5kW) - OptiMOS MOSFETs with XMC1000 MCUs for cost-sensitive applications like pumps and fans; Medium Power (5-75kW) - EconoDUAL IGBT modules with XMC4000 MCUs for standard industrial drives, servo systems, and compressors; High Power (75-500kW) - PrimePACK IGBT modules with AURIX TC2xx MCUs for large industrial drives, traction systems, and heavy machinery; High Efficiency - CoolSiC MOSFETs for premium efficiency requirements in servo drives and high-performance applications. Control Complexity - XMC for standard V/f control, AURIX for field-oriented control (FOC) and advanced motor control algorithms. Each solution page provides detailed BOM recommendations, performance specifications, and application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Click on Motor Drive Solutions to explore specific configurations, or contact our FAE team for customized motor drive recommendations.

faqs.question: What solutions does Infineon offer for renewable energy applications?
faqs.answer: Infineon provides comprehensive solutions for renewable energy: Solar Inverters - String inverters (3-20kW) using CoolSiC MOSFETs for maximum efficiency, central inverters (100kW+) using PrimePACK IGBT modules; Wind Power - Onshore and offshore wind converters using high-power IGBT modules and AURIX MCUs for grid synchronization; Energy Storage - Battery inverters and bidirectional converters using SiC MOSFETs for high-efficiency charge/discharge cycles; EV Charging - DC fast chargers using CoolSiC MOSFETs for compact, high-efficiency designs. Key Technologies - CoolSiC MOSFETs enable 99%+ efficiency and high switching frequencies reducing passive component size; PrimePACK modules provide proven reliability for utility-scale installations. All solutions include grid-tie compliance features and meet relevant safety standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore Solar Inverter or Wind Power solutions for detailed product recommendations and technical specifications.

faqs.question: How do Infineon solutions address automotive electrification?
faqs.answer: Infineon offers comprehensive solutions for automotive electrification: Traction Inverters - High-power IGBT modules and CoolSiC MOSFETs for electric vehicle main drives, delivering 50-250kW output power; Battery Management Systems (BMS) - AURIX TC3xx MCUs with ASIL-D safety certification for precise cell monitoring and balancing; Onboard Chargers (OBC) - CoolSiC MOSFETs for compact, high-efficiency AC-DC conversion; DC-DC Converters - Isolated converters for auxiliary systems using high-frequency switching devices; EV Charging - Solutions for both onboard and offboard charging infrastructure. Safety Features - All automotive solutions meet ISO 26262 functional safety requirements and AEC-Q100/Q101 qualification. Infineon's automotive portfolio enables complete electric powertrain design with proven reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore EV-specific solutions to find detailed product recommendations for traction, charging, and battery management applications.

faqs.question: What are the benefits of using Infineon reference designs?
faqs.answer: Infineon reference designs accelerate product development: Proven Performance - Designs validated by Infineon engineers with documented test results and efficiency measurements; Complete Documentation - Schematics, PCB layouts, BOMs, and application notes provided; Optimized Pairings - Components selected and tested to work together optimally (IGBTs with gate drivers, MCUs with sensors); Reduced Risk - Eliminates guesswork in component selection and circuit design; Faster Time-to-Market - Start with working design and customize for specific requirements; Cost Optimization - BOM optimized for performance and cost based on Infineon's extensive application experience. Reference designs cover motor drives, power supplies, renewable energy, and automotive applications. BeiLuo Electronics can provide reference design kits and technical support for implementation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us to request reference design documentation and evaluation kits for your target application.

faqs.question: How do I select the right solution for my power supply design?
faqs.answer: Power supply solution selection criteria: Power Level - <1kW: OptiMOS MOSFETs for DC-DC converters; 1-10kW: CoolMOS superjunction MOSFETs for PFC and LLC converters; >10kW: IGBT modules or CoolSiC MOSFETs for high-power applications; Topology - PFC stages: CoolMOS or CoolSiC for bridgeless designs; DC-DC stages: Select based on voltage and frequency requirements; Efficiency Target - Standard (90-94%): CoolMOS or IGBT solutions; Premium (95-99%): CoolSiC MOSFETs for highest efficiency; Input Voltage - 90-264VAC universal: Standard solutions; 380-480VAC three-phase: High-voltage IGBT or SiC solutions; Output Requirements - Isolated or non-isolated, voltage regulation, current sharing for parallel operation. Each solution page provides topology recommendations, efficiency curves, and detailed BOMs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse power supply solutions by topology and power level, or contact our FAE team for custom power supply design assistance.

solutions.title: EV Charger Solution
solutions.fullTitle: Complete EV Charging Station Solution with Infineon Power Semiconductors
solutions.description: Complete powertrain solution for electric vehicle chargers with high efficiency and compact design. Our EV charging solution delivers fast, reliable charging with industry-leading power density.
solutions.longDescription:
This comprehensive EV charging station solution leverages Infineon's complete portfolio of power semiconductors to deliver industry-leading efficiency, power density, and reliability. The solution supports AC Level 2 charging (up to 22kW) and DC fast charging (up to 150kW) configurations, making it suitable for residential, commercial, and public charging infrastructure.

The power stage utilizes Infineon's latest IGBT modules (FF300R12ME4) for the main inverter, combined with CoolSiC MOSFETs (IMW120R040M1H) for the high-frequency DC-DC conversion stage. This hybrid approach optimizes efficiency across the entire operating range while minimizing system cost. The gate drive stage employs EICEDRIVER ICs (1ED020I12-F2) with integrated protection features for reliable operation.

The control system is built around the Infineon XMC4700 industrial MCU, which provides the processing power needed for advanced control algorithms, communication interfaces, and safety monitoring. The XMC4700's integrated analog peripherals, including high-resolution ADCs and PWM generators, simplify the design while enabling precise control of the power stages.

Key features include power factor correction (PFC) for grid compliance, galvanic isolation for safety, comprehensive protection (over-current, over-voltage, over-temperature, ground fault), communication interfaces (CAN, Ethernet, PLC for smart grid integration), and compliance with CHAdeMO, CCS, and GB/T charging standards.

bomList.function: Main inverter switches for DC output stage
IGBT and MOSFET gate drive with protection
Output current sensing for protection and metering

technicalSpecs.protection: OCP, OVP, OTP, GFCI, Surge protection
technicalSpecs.standards: IEC 61851, UL 2202, CE, CHAdeMO, CCS, GB/T

solutions.seoTitle: EV Charger Solution | Infineon IGBT, SiC, MCU | Complete Design | BeiLuo
solutions.seoDescription: Complete EV charging station solution using Infineon IGBT modules, CoolSiC MOSFETs, gate drivers, and XMC MCU. >96% efficiency, 50-150kW. BOM, schematics, design support. Contact: +86 15013702378

faqs.question: What are the key design considerations for implementing a high-efficiency EV charger?
faqs.answer: EV charger design with Infineon components requires attention to: Topology Selection - AC Level 2 chargers use single-phase PFC with isolated DC-DC; DC fast chargers use three-phase Vienna rectifier or active front-end (AFE) with high-frequency DC-DC stage; Power Device Selection - Use CoolSiC MOSFETs (IMW120R040M1H) for PFC and DC-DC stages to achieve >96% efficiency and reduce cooling requirements; IGBT modules (FF300R12ME4) for high-power output stages; Thermal Management - Liquid cooling recommended for DC fast chargers >50kW; air cooling sufficient for AC Level 2 chargers; Control Architecture - XMC4700 MCU handles PFC control, DC-DC regulation, and charging protocol management; Safety & Compliance - Implement comprehensive protection (OCP, OVP, OTP, GFCI) and meet IEC 61851, UL 2202 standards; Communication - Support OCPP for smart grid integration and user authentication.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for the complete EV charger reference design including schematics, PCB layout, and control firmware.

faqs.question: How does the hybrid IGBT + SiC approach optimize EV charger performance?
faqs.answer: The hybrid approach combines the strengths of both technologies: IGBT for Output Stage - FF300R12ME4 IGBT modules provide cost-effective high-current switching for the final DC output stage where switching frequency is lower (<20kHz); CoolSiC for PFC and DC-DC - IMW120R040M1H SiC MOSFETs enable high-frequency operation (50-100kHz) in the PFC and isolated DC-DC stages, reducing magnetic component size by 30-50%; Efficiency Optimization - SiC devices have near-zero reverse recovery, eliminating switching losses in hard-switched PFC topologies; Cost Balance - Hybrid approach delivers 95-96% system efficiency at lower cost than all-SiC designs; Thermal Benefits - Lower switching losses reduce cooling requirements, enabling compact designs. This architecture is ideal for DC fast chargers (50-150kW) where efficiency, power density, and cost must be balanced.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request our EV charger BOM with hybrid IGBT+SiC architecture for optimal cost-performance balance.

faqs.question: What safety certifications are required for EV charging stations?
faqs.answer: EV charging stations require multiple safety certifications: Electrical Safety - IEC 61851 (international), UL 2202 (North America), CE marking (Europe) for electrical safety and EMC compliance; Grid Connection - Compliance with local grid codes for harmonics (IEC 61000), power factor, and anti-islanding; Communication Protocols - CHAdeMO, CCS (Combo), GB/T for DC fast charging; OCPP 1.6/2.0 for backend communication; Functional Safety - ISO 13849 for machinery safety; SIL 2/3 ratings for protection systems; Environmental - IP54 minimum for outdoor installations; IK08 for mechanical impact resistance; Fire Safety - Compliance with UL 94 V-0 for flammability; thermal runaway protection for battery systems. Our EV charger solution includes pre-certified design elements and documentation to accelerate your certification process.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our automotive FAE team for detailed safety concept documentation and certification support for EV charger designs.

solutions.name: Industrial Control Solution 1

solutions.title: Industrial Motor Drive
solutions.fullTitle: High-Performance Industrial Motor Drive Solution with Infineon IGBT & MCU
solutions.description: High-performance motor drive solution for industrial applications. Precise speed and torque control with energy efficiency.
solutions.longDescription:
This industrial motor drive solution provides complete control of AC induction motors, permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSM), and brushless DC (BLDC) motors in industrial applications. The solution supports power ratings from 7.5kW to 75kW, making it suitable for pumps, fans, conveyors, machine tools, and robotics applications.

The power stage utilizes Infineon's EconoDUAL 3 IGBT modules (FF600R12ME4 for higher power, FF300R12ME4 for lower power) with optimized switching characteristics for motor drive applications. The IGBT modules feature low saturation voltage and fast switching to minimize losses while maintaining excellent short-circuit ruggedness.

The control system is implemented using the Infineon XMC4700 industrial MCU, which provides the computational power needed for advanced motor control algorithms including field-oriented control (FOC), direct torque control (DTC), and sensorless control. The XMC4700's integrated high-resolution ADCs, PWM generators, and position interface (QDEC) simplify the design while enabling precise motor control.

Key features include sensorless startup and operation, field weakening for extended speed range, built-in PID controllers for process control, comprehensive protection (over-current, over-voltage, over-temperature, motor stall), communication interfaces (Modbus RTU/TCP, PROFINET, EtherCAT options), and compliance with IEC 61800 motor drive standards.

IGBT gate drive with desaturation protection

technicalSpecs.protection: OCP, OVP, OTP, UVLO, Motor stall, Ground fault

faeInsights.insight: In my 15 years of supporting motor drive applications, I've learned that successful inverter design requires careful attention to three key aspects: thermal management, EMI mitigation, and protection mechanisms. The Infineon IGBT modules we recommend for motor drives have proven their reliability in countless industrial installations worldwide. What sets them apart is the optimal balance between conduction and switching losses, which directly impacts system efficiency and cooling requirements. For high-power applications, I always emphasize the importance of proper gate drive design - the switching characteristics of your IGBT are only as good as your gate driver allows. Modern motor drives also need to consider harmonic distortion and grid compliance, which affects filter design and switching frequency selection.
faeInsights.logic: When designing a motor drive solution, follow this decision framework: 1) Determine motor power and voltage requirements; 2) Calculate DC bus voltage and select appropriate IGBT voltage rating (typically 1.5-2x DC bus); 3) Size IGBT current rating with 1.5-2x safety margin for overload conditions; 4) Evaluate thermal requirements based on switching frequency and cooling method; 5) Design gate drive circuit with proper protection features; 6) Implement current sensing and protection circuits; 7) Validate design through comprehensive testing including overload, short-circuit, and thermal cycling.
keyTakeaways: Always include 50-100% current margin for motor starting and overload conditions
Thermal design is critical - calculate losses at worst-case operating points
Gate drive design significantly impacts switching performance and reliability
EMI filtering requirements increase with switching frequency
Protection features (overcurrent, overtemperature, undervoltage) are essential
commonPitfalls: Undersizing the IGBT module for motor starting current (5-7x rated current)
Inadequate thermal design leading to overheating under continuous operation
Poor layout causing excessive stray inductance and voltage overshoot
bestPractices: Use Infineon's recommended gate drivers with integrated protection
Implement soft-start to limit inrush current during motor starting
Include DC bus pre-charge circuit for drives above 5kW
Design for worst-case thermal conditions (maximum ambient, full load)
Use isolated current sensors for motor phase current measurement

solutions.name: Automotive Electronics Solution 2

solutions.title: Solar Inverter
solutions.fullTitle: High-Efficiency Solar String Inverter Solution with Infineon SiC & IGBT
solutions.description: High-efficiency solar inverter solution for photovoltaic systems. Maximum power point tracking and grid compliance.
solutions.longDescription:
This solar string inverter solution delivers industry-leading efficiency for residential and commercial photovoltaic (PV) systems. The solution supports power ratings from 10kW to 50kW, with MPPT (Maximum Power Point Tracking) efficiency exceeding 99.9% and European efficiency up to 98.5%. The design leverages Infineon's complete portfolio of power semiconductors optimized for solar applications.

The DC-DC boost stage utilizes Infineon CoolSiC MOSFETs (IMW120R040M1H) to achieve high switching frequency operation with minimal losses. Silicon carbide technology enables higher efficiency compared to traditional silicon devices, particularly at partial load conditions common in solar applications. The DC-AC inverter stage employs Infineon IGBT modules (FF300R12ME4) for reliable grid-tied operation.

The control system is built around the Infineon XMC4700 industrial MCU, which provides the processing power for advanced MPPT algorithms, grid monitoring, and communication functions. The XMC4700's integrated high-resolution ADCs enable precise current and voltage measurement for accurate power control.

Key features include dual MPPT inputs for flexible array configuration, integrated DC disconnect, anti-islanding protection, ground fault detection, arc fault detection (AFCI), grid support functions (low voltage ride-through, reactive power control), and communication interfaces (RS485, Ethernet, Wi-Fi, cellular options) for remote monitoring.

bomList.function: DC-AC inverter stage for grid connection
SiC MOSFET and IGBT gate drive

technicalSpecs.protection: Anti-islanding, GFCI, AFCI, OVP, OCP, Reverse polarity

faeInsights.insight: Solar inverter design presents unique challenges compared to motor drives. The input voltage varies widely with solar irradiance and temperature, requiring power devices that can operate efficiently across a broad range. I've seen many designs fail because they were optimized only for nominal conditions, not the full operating envelope. Efficiency is paramount in solar applications - every 0.1% improvement translates to significant energy harvest over the system lifetime. Modern solar inverters also need sophisticated grid support functions, which requires fast and accurate control algorithms. The choice between IGBT and SiC MOSFET is becoming increasingly important as efficiency targets rise above 98%.
faeInsights.logic: Solar inverter design process: 1) Define DC input voltage range based on panel configuration and climate conditions; 2) Select DC-AC topology (H-bridge, NPC, T-type) based on voltage and efficiency requirements; 3) Choose power devices considering both cost and efficiency targets; 4) Design MPPT algorithm and control loop for maximum energy harvest; 5) Implement grid synchronization and protection per local standards; 6) Design thermal management for outdoor operation; 7) Validate efficiency across full operating range.
keyTakeaways: Design for full DC voltage range, not just nominal conditions
Efficiency at partial load is as important as full-load efficiency
SiC devices offer significant advantages for high-frequency operation
Grid codes vary by region - ensure compliance with local requirements
Reliability is critical - 20+ year expected lifetime
commonPitfalls: Optimizing only for full-load efficiency, ignoring light-load performance
Poor thermal design for outdoor temperature extremes
bestPractices: Use wide-bandgap devices (SiC/GaN) for highest efficiency
Implement multi-MPPT for systems with partial shading
Design for 100% overload capability during grid transients
Include arc fault detection for fire safety
Use conformal coating for outdoor environmental protection

solutions.name: Consumer Electronics Solution 3

solutions.title: Wind Power Converter
solutions.fullTitle: Wind Turbine Power Converter Solution with Infineon IGBT Modules
solutions.description: Robust wind power converter solution for turbine applications. Reliable operation in harsh environments.
solutions.longDescription:
This wind power converter solution is designed for demanding wind turbine applications, providing reliable power conversion in harsh environmental conditions. The solution supports both doubly-fed induction generator (DFIG) and full-scale converter configurations for wind turbines ranging from 1.5MW to 5MW rated power.

The power stage utilizes Infineon's high-power IGBT modules in PrimePACK and IHV packages, specifically selected for wind power applications. These modules feature enhanced thermal cycling capability, low failure rates, and extended lifetime to match the 20+ year operational requirements of wind turbines. The modules incorporate Infineon's latest trench/field-stop IGBT technology for optimal efficiency and ruggedness.

The control system employs Infineon's industrial-grade components with extended temperature range operation (-40°C to +85°C ambient). Redundant control architectures are supported for maximum availability. The system includes comprehensive monitoring and diagnostics for predictive maintenance.

Key features include low voltage ride-through (LVRT) capability for grid code compliance, reactive power control for grid support, active damping for mechanical resonance suppression, pitch control integration, comprehensive condition monitoring, and remote diagnostics capabilities.

technicalSpecs.protection: LVRT, OVP, OCP, OTP, Grid fault detection

solutions.name: Communication Systems Solution 4

solutions.title: Rail Transit Traction
solutions.fullTitle: Rail Transit Traction Converter Solution with Infineon IGBT Modules
solutions.description: High-power traction converter solution for rail transit applications. EN 50155 compliant for railway standards.
solutions.longDescription:
This rail transit traction converter solution is specifically designed for electric trains, metros, light rail vehicles, and high-speed rail applications. The solution provides reliable power conversion for traction motor drives, meeting the stringent requirements of railway standards including EN 50155 (electronic equipment for rolling stock) and EN 50121 (electromagnetic compatibility).

The power stage utilizes Infineon's high-power IGBT modules specifically qualified for traction applications. These modules feature enhanced thermal cycling capability to withstand the demanding load profiles of rail transit, including frequent acceleration and braking cycles. The modules incorporate Infineon's latest IGBT technology for optimal efficiency and ruggedness.

The control system employs redundant architectures for maximum safety and availability. The system includes comprehensive protection features to ensure safe operation under all conditions, including overcurrent, overvoltage, overtemperature, and ground fault protection. Advanced features include regenerative braking for energy recovery, power factor correction, and harmonic filtering.

Key features include EN 50155 compliance for rolling stock equipment, wide input voltage range (DC 600V to DC 3000V for various rail electrification systems), high power density for space-constrained installations, comprehensive diagnostics and condition monitoring, and predictive maintenance capabilities.

faqs.question: What are the system integration considerations?
faqs.answer: When integrating this undefined into your system: (1) Power supply design - ensure adequate capacity and stability for all components. (6) Testing - validate system performance under all operating conditions.
